content.title: 问题 (wèntí) — Question/Problem
content.body:
**问题** (wèntí) can mean both "question" and "problem" depending on context. The character 问 means "to ask" and 题 means "topic" or "subject."

As "question": 你有问题吗？ (nǐ yǒu wèntí ma?) — Do you have any questions?

As "problem": 没问题 (méi wèntí) — No problem.

content.title: 学生 (xuésheng) — Student
content.body:
**学生** (xuésheng) means "student." The character 学 means "learn" and 生 means "person" or "life," so literally it means "learning person."

Example: 我是学生 (wǒ shì xuésheng) — I am a student.

content.title: 电脑 (diànnǎo) — Computer
content.body:
**电脑** (diànnǎo) means "computer." The character 电 means "electricity" and 脑 means "brain," so it literally translates to "electric brain."

Example: 我有电脑 (wǒ yǒu diànnǎo) — I have a computer.

content.title: 汉语 (hànyǔ) — Chinese Language
content.body:
**汉语** (hànyǔ) specifically refers to the Chinese language, particularly Mandarin. 汉 (hàn) refers to the Han ethnicity (the majority ethnic group in China), and 语 (yǔ) means "language."

Example: 我学习汉语 (wǒ xuéxí hànyǔ) — I study Chinese.

This is the standard term used in mainland China for the Chin

content.title: Cross-Cultural Professional Context
content.body:
In Chinese professional settings, addressing people correctly matters:

- Teachers are often called 老师 even without their surname
- Doctors use their surname + 医生: 王医生 (Dr. Wang)
- Introducing yourself includes your profession: 我是... + profession
- Asking about someone's work: 你工作吗？ is a simple yes/

content.title: 工作 (gōngzuò) — Work/Job
content.body:
The word **工作** (gōngzuò) means both "work" and "job" in Mandarin. It can function as a noun or a verb.

As a noun: 我有工作 (wǒ yǒu gōngzuò) — I have a job.

As a verb: 我工作 (wǒ gōngzuò) — I work.

The character 工 originally depicted a carpenter's square, symbolizing labor and work.

content.title: Daily Routine Vocabulary Set
content.body:
These HSK 1 words commonly appear together when discussing daily routines:

**Professions and roles:**
- 老师 (lǎoshī) — teacher
- 学生 (xuésheng) — student
- 医生 (yīshēng) — doctor

**Activities:**
- 工作 (gōngzuò) — work/to work
- 学习 (xuéxí) — study/to study

**Languages:**
- 汉语 (hànyǔ) — Chinese languag

content.title: 学习 (xuéxí) — Study/Learn
content.body:
**学习** (xuéxí) means "to study" or "to learn." It's a common verb used for educational activities.

Examples:
- 我学习汉语 (wǒ xuéxí hànyǔ) — I study Chinese.
- 他学习英语 (tā xuéxí yīngyǔ) — He studies English.

The first character 学 means "learn" and the second 习 means "practice."

content.title: 英语 (yīngyǔ) — English Language
content.body:
**英语** (yīngyǔ) means "English language." The character 英 (yīng) is a phonetic representation of "English," and 语 (yǔ) means "language."

Example: 他学习英语 (tā xuéxí yīngyǔ) — He studies English.

content.title: Daily Routine Description
content.body:
我叫李华。我是学生。我学习汉语和英语。我有书、电脑和电话。我的老师叫王老师。我有问题，我问老师。

(My name is Li Hua. I am a student. I study Chinese and English. I have books, a computer, and a phone. My teacher is called Teacher Wang. I have questions, I ask the teacher.)

content.title: Work Context Comprehension
content.body:
张医生工作。她是医生。她有电脑和电话。她的学生学习医学。学生有问题，问张医生。

(Dr. Zhang works. She is a doctor. She has a computer and phone. Her students study medicine. When students have questions, they ask Dr. Zhang.)

content.title: Professional Identity
content.body:
Three key profession words at HSK 1 level all follow the pattern of ending with a character related to "person":

- 老**师** (lǎoshī) — teacher (老 = respected + 师 = master)
- 学**生** (xuésheng) — student (学 = study + 生 = person)
- 医**生** (yīshēng) — doctor (医 = medicine + 生 = person)

When stating your

content.title: Language Learning Context
content.body:
When talking about language learning, you use 学习 (study) + language name:

- 我学习**汉语** (wǒ xuéxí hànyǔ) — I study Chinese
- 她学习**英语** (tā xuéxí yīngyǔ) — She studies English

Both 汉语 and 英语 end with 语 (yǔ), meaning "language." This character appears in many language names.

Common pattern: [culture/

content.title: Vocabulary Application in Context
content.body:
When describing your daily routine or work, you can combine these words naturally:

**Morning routine:** 我是学生。我学习汉语。我有书和电脑。

**Work context:** 我工作。我是老师。学生有问题，我...

**Study context:** 我学习英语和汉语。我有老师。老师的名字是...

Notice how professions, objects, and activities connect logically.

content.title: 老师 (lǎoshī) — Teacher
content.body:
**老师** (lǎoshī) means "teacher." It's a respectful term used for educators at all levels. 老 (lǎo) means "old" (conveying respect), and 师 (shī) means "master" or "teacher."

You can address teachers directly as 老师 without using their name: 老师好！ (lǎoshī hǎo!) — Hello, teacher!

content.title: Work and Study Contexts
content.body:
When discussing work and study, certain word patterns are common:

**Identity statements with 是:**
- 我是学生 (wǒ shì xuésheng) — I am a student
- 她是老师 (tā shì lǎoshī) — She is a teacher
- 他是医生 (tā shì yīshēng) — He is a doctor

**Activity statements (no 是):**
- 我学习汉语 (wǒ xuéxí hànyǔ) — I study Chinese

content.title: 医生 (yīshēng) — Doctor
content.body:
**医生** (yīshēng) means "doctor" or "physician." The character 医 means "medicine" or "to heal," and 生 means "person," so it literally means "medicine person."

Example: 他是医生 (tā shì yīshēng) — He is a doctor.

content.title: 书 (shū) — Book
content.body:
**书** (shū) means "book." It's a simple, single-character word for any kind of book.

Examples:
- 我有书 (wǒ yǒu shū) — I have a book/books.
- 我看书 (wǒ kàn shū) — I read books.

content.title: 电话 (diànhuà) — Telephone/Phone Call
content.body:
**电话** (diànhuà) means "telephone" or "phone call." The character 电 means "electricity" and 话 means "speech" or "words," so it literally means "electric speech."

It can refer to the device or the action of calling:
- 我有电话 (wǒ yǒu diànhuà) — I have a phone.
- 打电话 (dǎ diànhuà) — to make a phone call

content.title: Simple Self-Introduction
content.body:
A basic self-introduction in Mandarin might include:

你好！我叫[name]。我是学生。我学习汉语。

(Hello! My name is [name]. I am a student. I study Chinese.)

For work contexts:
我叫[name]。我是老师/医生。我工作。

This uses: 叫 (be called), 是 (am), 学习 (study), 工作 (work), plus profession and language vocabulary.

content.title: 名字 (míngzi) — Name
content.body:
**名字** (míngzi) means "name." The character 名 means "name" or "famous," and 字 also means "character" or "word."

Common question: 你叫什么名字？ (nǐ jiào shénme míngzi?) — What is your name?

Answer: 我叫... (wǒ jiào...) — My name is...

content.title: Technology and Communication
content.body:
The "electric" (电) words form a common pattern in Mandarin for modern technology:

- **电话** (diànhuà) — telephone (electric + speech)
- **电脑** (diànnǎo) — computer (electric + brain)

Both words share 电 (electricity) and combine it with traditional concepts:
- 话 (huà) = speech/words
- 脑 (nǎo) = brai
